channel.title: SEAtoday - Food & Drink
channel.description: Upcoming events for SEAtoday - Food & Drink

item.title: Goose Ridge: Library Sale & Tasting
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Goose Ridge Woodinville Tasting Room, Goose Ridge Estate Winery-Woodinville Tasting Room, 14450 Woodinville Redmond Rd NE, Woodinville, WA

Join us for our Library Sale & Tasting, beginning February 27th and available for a limited time while supplies last. We’re opening the cellar and bringing back select library wines for a special tasting and sale at Goose Ridge Woodinville. Enjoy the opportunity to purchase beautifully aged wines from past vintages. Whether you’re looking to restock a favorite vintage or discover a hidden gem from our cellar, this is the perfect time to explore our library collection. Inventory is limited. Available while supplies last.

Event details

item.title: THE BLOCK: Mobile Cafe Meet-up
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: FAIRVIEW MARKET HALL, 400 Fairview Avenue North, Seattle, WA

THE BLOCK brings together Seattle’s standout mobile cafés for a day of bold flavors, cultural creativity, and community, all on one block.

Event details

item.title: HAPPY HOUR FROM OPENING UNTIL 5:00 PM
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: 2446 NW Market St. Seattle, WA, 9810, 2446 NW Market St. Seattle, WA, 9810, Seattle, WA

HAPPY HOUR FROM OPENING UNTIL 5:00 PM MONDAY TOTHURSDAY HOT PIZZA-COLD BEER-GOOD VIBES COOL GAMES 2446 NW MARKET ST.SEATTLE, WA, 98107 20% OFF WWW.BALLARDBEERBOX.COM

Event details

item.title: Get 15% OFF when you order 10+ pizzas or salads between Mon-Thu, 2-6 PM!
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: 2446 NW Market St. Seattle, WA, 98107, 2446 NW Market St, Seattle, WA

Event details

item.title: Coffee Connections (March)
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Active Mill Creek, 122 128th St SE, Everett, WA

March’s COFFEE CONNECTIONS is HOSTED at: Active Martial Arts Address: 122 128th St SE, Everett, WA 98208 Please join your fellow Mill Creek Chamber members and guests to our monthly member-hosted event where businesses get to network, learn about each other, and connect on a personal and casual level! Guests welcome also! Always an exciting new Mill Creek Chamber event; we’ll be hosted at new location each month, so if you have a venue that you’d like to gain more exposure from, please let us know! If you’re a guest, we welcome you to learn more about the benefits of membership. When available, our Chamber Director will be present to discuss, and when not; Networking Committee members can assist as well. Have some coffee, make some connections. • March 10th, 2026 • 8am-9:30am

Event details

item.title: Coffee Connections
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Active Martial Arts Mill Creek, 122 128th St SE, Ste B, Everett, WA

March's COFFEE CONNECTIONS is HOSTED at: Active Martial Arts Address: 122 128th St SE, Everett, WA 98208 Please join your fellow Mill Creek Chamber members and guests to our monthly member-hosted event where businesses get to network, learn about each other, and connect on a personal and casual level! Guests welcome also! Always an exciting new Mill Creek Chamber event; we’ll be hosted at new location each month, so if you have a venue that you’d like to gain more exposure from, please let us know! If you’re a guest, we welcome you to learn more about the benefits of membership. When available, our Chamber Director will be present to discuss, and when not; Networking Committee members can assist as well. Have some coffee, make some connections. • March 10th, 2026 • 8am-9:30am

Event details

item.title: Coworking Tuesdays
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Bothell Kenmore Chamber, 10031 Main Street, Suite A, Bothell, WA

Tuesday Cowork \+ Coffee/Tea \(Weekly\) Need a reason to get out of your inbox cave and into a productive, low-pressure space? Join us every Tuesday for a drop-in coworking morning—bring your laptop, knock out your priorities, and enjoy the energy of working alongside other local professionals. When: Tuesdays | 9:30–11:30 AM | No registration needed Format: Come for the full two hours or pop in when you can Who it’s for: Business owners, remote workers, creatives, students, nonprofit leaders—anyone who wants focused work time \+ light community What to expect: Quiet “get-it-done” work blocks, optional hellos \+ quick intros, and a friendly vibe \(no forced networking\) Location \(through February\): Bothell Visitor Center We’ll rotate locations monthly—same time, same Tuesday rhythm, fresh spaces. Bring: Your laptop \+ whatever you need to work. We have the coffee, tea, and water! Questions? Email sandra@bothellkenmorechamber.org

Event details

item.title: Tea with T: Informational Webinar on WEBS
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Description Tea with T: How to Use WEBS Tuesday, March 10 | 10:30 AM PT | Zoom Join GSBA for Tea with T, an informational webinar led by Toraya Miller, where we'll walk through Washington's Electronic Business Solution \(WEBS\) and how to register and use it to find state contracting opportunities. Bring a cup of tea and get set up in 5-10 minutes so bid opportunities that match your commodity codes can start coming straight to your inbox. What is WEBS? Washington's Electronic Business Solution \(WEBS\) is an internet-based bid notification system hosted by the Department of Enterprise Services \(DES\). Many state agencies and educational institutions use WEBS to notify businesses of contracting opportunities based on the commodity codes you select. About Toraya Miller Director of Training & Consulting, GSBA Toraya Miller leads GSBA's training and consulting work, supporting businesses across Washington as they navigate growth, certification, and public-sector opportunities. In this session, Toraya will guide participants through WEBS and share practical tips for getting registered, staying updated, and using the system to identify contracting opportunities with the state. In this webinar, we'll cover: ☕ What WEBS is and why it matters for vendors ☕ How to register as a vendor in WEBS ☕ How to choose and update your commodity codes ☕ Where to find current and upcoming bid opportunities ☕ How to use forecasted purchases to plan ahead ☕ Tips to keep your profile updated so opportunities keep brewing Learn more about WEBS Washington Department of Enterprise Services: Bid Opportunities \+ WEBS

Event details

item.title: Exceptional Connections March In-Person Networking Luncheon
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Fogo de Chão Brazilian Steakhouse, 18602 Alderwood Mall Parkway, #Suite 1110, Lynnwood, WA

Imagine a world where people naturally contribute to each other, knowing that when we are in service to others, we transform lives.

Event details

item.title: Break Even Bottle Program - Every Tuesday at The Cottage!
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: The Cottage, 10029 NE 183rd St, Bothell, WA

Discover something truly exceptional every Tuesday at The Cottage with our Break Even Bottle program, where rare and exquisite spirits are yours to explore. 📅 When: Every Tuesday 4:00 PM to 9:00 PM \(or until the bottle is finished\) 📍 Where: The Cottage – Bothell Each week, we open a unique and rare bottle, offering 1oz pours at cost. It’s your chance to try something extraordinary that you won’t find on most menus—perfect for seasoned connoisseurs and curious tasters alike. Why Join Us? ✨ Exclusive access to rare and premium spirits ✨ Affordable 1oz pours ✨ A cozy and inviting atmosphere Bring your friends and savor an evening of exceptional flavors. Don’t miss out on this unique tasting experience! Reservations are encouraged: https://resy.com/cities/bothell-wa/venues/the-cottage-bothell?date=2025-01-15&seats=2 Spread the word and join us for the Break Even Bottle program at The Cottage. See you Tuesday!

Event details

item.title: What's Brewing with Ed?
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Four Generals Brewing, 229 Wells Avenue South, Renton, WA

Please note: In March, this event will take place on the second Tuesday, March 10. Join Renton Chief Administrative Officer Ed VanValey and city leadership for conversations! This free monthly event, open to all ages, gives you the opportunity to learn about city projects and initiatives and ask questions! Tuesday, March 10 5:30 – 6:30 p.m. Four Generals Brewing \(229 Wells Ave. S.\) Please RSVP here on Facebook to help staff and Four Generals plan. You can also RSVP by emailing communications@rentonwa.gov Learn more: rentonwa.gov/whatsbrewing

Event details

item.title: Eastside Coffee Outside Every Wednesday Morning
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 7 AM

Location: Rotary Central Station Picnic Shelter, 1 Railroad Avenue, Kirkland, WA

Eastside Coffee Outside Every Wednesday Morning Drop by to join us ANY Wednesday, rain or shine, for a chat, a snack, and something hot to drink. No RSVP necessary. You'll find us Every Wednesday, 7:00 AM to 9:30 AM Rotary Central Station \(the picnic shelter\) 1 Railroad Ave, Kirkland, WA 98033 Anna brings her fresh-baked vegan muffins to share. Dylan often brews different varieties of loose-leaf tea to share. Kurt brews regular and decaf coffee and brings hot water, tea bags, and hot cocoa packets. Bring your pup for a treat, too! We're here from 7-9:30am, but you can stop by for as much or as little of that time as works for you. It's on the Cross Kirkland Corridor, so it's easily accessible for walkers, joggers, bikers, and by car. There are reusable mugs available, or bring your own. Hope to see you ANY or EVERY Wednesday morning!

Event details

item.title: Met Market
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 11 AM

Get ready for warm, buttery, jam-filled goodness. The Fisher Scones truck is serving up a Pacific Northwest favorite that has been loved since 1911. Grab a fresh-baked Original Fisher Scone, flaky on the outside, soft and jammy on the inside. Perfect for a snack, a treat, or sharing with friends and family.

Event details

item.title: Joint Chamber Breakfast
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 8 AM

Join us for a Joint Chamber Breakfast hosted by the Mill Creek Chamber of Commerce.   Partnering Chambers Building Relationships: Woodinville, Bothell Kenmore & Mill Creek. 📅 Date: March 12 📍 Location: Tapped Public House 🕒 Time: 8:00 AM – 9:30 AM 📩 RSVP: HERE Agenda: 8am: Registration, Networking & Breakfast 8:20am: Welcome & Announcements 8:30am: Engagement Activities & Door Prizes 9:30am: Event Concludes Prices: $23 – Member of a Partnering Chamber $28 – Non Member

Event details

item.title: 2026 March Morning Mix & Mingle
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Seattle Southside Chamber of Commerce, 3100 S 176th St Suite 210, SeaTac, WA

Join us for our March Mix & Mingle! Come sip coffee or tea, exchange ideas, and business cards, and meet like-minded businesses at the Chamber's headquarters. A special thank you to our incredible speakers and sponsors for making this event possible! Plus, get to know two amazing Seattle Southside Chamber Ambassadors who will be joining us. Don't miss out on the chance to explore exciting business involvement opportunities, including sponsorships. Let's grow and thrive together! BUSINESS INVOLVEMENT OPPORTUNITIES: Sponsorship opportunities are still available, for more information please download our Seattle Southside Chamber Event Sponsorship Form.

Event details

item.title: March Luncheon: Practical Business AI Tools
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Youngtown Cultural Arts Center, 4408 Delridge Way Sw, Seattle, WA

Description This luncheon features a panel conversation led by SBDC experts with a focus on real world AI tools that support daily business needs. Attendees will hear clear examples, walk through practical how to moments, and take part in an open forum where questions and curiosities will shape our discussion. It will be an accessible, hands on look at AI that helps West Seattle businesses work smarter, not harder. Bring your questions and your laptops! Explore new horizons and connect with fellow professionals at the West Seattle Chamber&'s monthly luncheons, where engaging discussions and networking opportunities lay the groundwork for you to grow your business.

Event details

item.title: March Luncheon
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Youngstown Cultural Arts Center, 4408 Delridge Wy SW, Seattle, WA

Description Explore new horizons and connect with fellow professionals at the West Seattle Chamber's monthly luncheons, where engaging discussions and networking opportunities lay the groundwork for you to grow your business.

Event details

item.title: Artvana LUCKY Paint & Sip in Lower Queen Anne at Omeros Cafe March 2026
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Omeros Corporation, 201 Elliott Avenue West, Seattle, WA

Artvana is in Lower Queen Anne! Come and join us for fun and creativity with a Paint and Sip at Omeros Cafe!

Event details

item.title: All Night Happy Hour at The Cottage
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: The Cottage, 10029 NE 183rd St, Bothell, WA

Hey everyone, Are you looking for a fun way to unwind after a long day at work? Join us every Thursday evening from 4-9pm for our all-night happy hour event! We’ve got a great selection of refreshing drinks and delicious snacks featuring fresh seasonal flavors that you won’t want to miss. Whether you’re in the mood for a classic cocktail or something new and exciting, we’ve got you covered. So gather your friends and come join us for a night of good drinks, good food, and good company. We can’t wait to see you there! Don’t forget to book a reservation so we can reserve a spot for you. See you on Thursday! RESY: https://resy.com/cities/bol/the-cottage-bothell

Event details

item.title: FREE OFFICE AND COFFEE FRIDAYS!
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Spaces- Stadium, 450 Alaskan Way South #Suite 200, Seattle, WA

Join us for the day and treat yourself to one of our cozy, furnished office spaces. The coffee's on us!

Event details

item.title: Open Coworking Day at Birch Road
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Birch Road, 1212 Minor Avenue, Seattle, WA

We're not your typical co-working space and our members wouldn't have it any other way. ​ If you’re looking for a change of pace, a bit of peace and quiet, and a spot to call your own without being alone, you’ve found it. Experience the perfect blend of productivity and community at Birch Road Clubhouse. We open at 8 AM every day of the year with complimentary coffee, lightning-fast Wi-Fi, and an instant social circle waiting for you.  Come see if we’re your perfect fit. Join us for an Open Co-working Day at your local Birch Road Clubhouse to try the club out for yourself.

Event details

item.title: Feedme's 5th Annual Edmonds Oyster Fest
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Our five Edmonds locations: Bar Dojo, Fire & The Feast, Market Seafood Eatery, Salt & Iron, and SanKai., 8404 Bowdoin Way, Edmonds, WA

Feedme’s 5th Annual Edmonds Oyster Fest March 13–22, 2026 Ten days. Five Edmonds locations. Endless oysters. Oyster madness? You bet. Feedme’s Annual Edmonds Oyster Fest is back! Join us for ten days of oyster specials across our five Edmonds locations: Bar Dojo, Fire & The Feast, Market Seafood Eatery, Salt & Iron, and SanKai. Featuring oysters from Taylor Shellfish and private label wine pairings from The Winemakers Reserve. Regular menus available at all locations. Make it an oyster crawl with our Oyster Fest Punch Card. Each Oyster Fest item ordered earns one punch. Collect three punches across any of the five locations \(one punch per visit per location\) and receive a $20 gift card — first come, first served by location. Join the shuckin’ fun.

Event details

item.title: Live Music Happy Hour Joe Casalini Trio
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: The Royal Room, 5000 Rainier Ave South, Seattle, WA

Join us for Live Music Happy Hour with the Joe Casalini Trio. The Casalini Trio has played happy hours at the Royal Room since 2019. They play laid-back, swinging versions of great tunes by Duke Ellington, Billy Strayhorn, Hoagie Carmichael, Phillip Johnston, and other great American songwriters of the last 100 years. The happy hour usually lasts about an hour and a half. Good drinks, good people, good ambience, and there’s never a cover charge, although the band often encourages donations to local food banks and other worthy causes.

Event details

item.title: Highway 9 Bridge Planting Party
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 9 AM

Description Join us at this restoration site along South Skagit Highway near the Highway 9 bridge. From 9am-12pm we'll plant native trees and shrubs to restore riparian habitat at this riverside site. These plants will help restore the Skagit River and improve habitat for salmon and many other animals. Bring a club, group and all your friends- all ages welcome! Please dress for the weather, and wear shoes that can get muddy. We'll provide gloves, tools and hot drinks/snacks! Click the link to register today!

Event details

item.title: Cookie And Coffee Pairing
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 10 AM

Please join us in a coffee and cookie pairing where we have matched up your favorite Girl Scout Cookies with our favorite coffee drinks! for just $14 you will be able to sample Girl Scout cookies and four 4oz drinks! Troop 44323 will also be there selling cookies for their last booth ever!

Event details

item.title: La Cocina Cooking Class con Chef Garzón (Bad Chancla)
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: El Centro De La Raza, 2524 16th Ave S, Seattle, WA

La Cocina Cooking Class con Chef Garzón \(Bad Chancla\) Sunday, March 15, 2026 Turn pantry staples into comfort food with heart. Join us on March 15th from 12:00 pm to 2:00 pm for a hands-on cooking class with Bad Chancla, where you’ll learn how to make white rice, guisada beans, savory picadillo ground beef, and a bright salsa criolla using everyday ingredients. This class will also cover quick pickling, hybrid cooking \(using both canned and fresh vegetables\), and smart ways to stretch and elevate your pantry essentials, no fancy tools or ingredients required; just an apron if you have one! We will provide all the ingredients and there will be time at the end of the class to enjoy the delicious meal we create. All proceeds support our Senior Program, which provides healthy meals, a welcoming space, and social activities for elders Monday through Friday. Come cook, learn practical skills, and give back to our community, all in one delicious night!

Event details

item.title: Issaquah Drink N' Draw
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Formula Brewing, 1875 NW Poplar Way, Issaquah, WA

ISSAQUAH'S LOCAL ART MEETUP Calling all artists and art enthusiasts! Join us for an evening filled with creativity, camaraderie, and, of course, drinks at your local Issaquah Drink n' Draw! Location: Formula Brewing, 1875 NW Poplar Way, Issaquah, WA 98027 Indulge your artistic side in a relaxed and social setting. Whether you're a seasoned artist or just starting your creative journey, this event is the perfect opportunity to: Draw: Bring your sketchbooks, pencils, tablets, or any medium of your choice. As long as it can fit in a 2' x 2' space and can be cleaned up easily, we welcome art of all types! Unleash your imagination and create alongside like-minded individuals. Let your creativity flow! Don't know what to draw? Search the hashtag \#essdrawmonthly or find the latest pinned Prompt List on the ESS group page! ️ Chat: Engage in lively conversations with fellow artists. Share tips, techniques, and stories while getting inspired by each other's work. Connect with a diverse community passionate about art. Drink: Sip on your favorite beverages from Formula's selection of refreshments. Whether it's a cold beer, a glass of wine, or a non-alcoholic option, enjoy a drink while you immerse yourself in the creative process. ✏️ This event is open to artists of all skill levels and backgrounds. Feel free to join us solo or invite friends to share in this artistic experience. Admission is FREE. Seating is first-come, first-served. While there's usually enough table space, if we get too busy, you may have to spread out. Come and be a part of this vibrant artistic gathering! Let's sketch, chat, and create memorable art together. We can't wait to see your masterpieces! For more information, contact group organizers Blayne Fox and Kat Klockow. Note: Remember to drink responsibly and be mindful of others' space and creativity during the event.

Event details

item.title: Liveable Kirkland Meetup -- Project: Build Whistle Kits
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Chainline Brewing Company, 500 Urban Plz, Kirkland, WA

Liveable Kirkland Meetups Mon, Feb 16, Mar 2, Mar 16, 6-8pm Chainline Brewing at Kirkland Urban For this special series of Meetups, we'll kick off our usual socials with an ICE-breaker activity -- assembling whistle kits. These kits will contain a tiny whistle, a "Know Your Rights" card, and instructions on what to do and who to call if you see ICE Activity. After assembling whistle kits, we'll socialize as usual. We'll get to know one another and chat about local events and issues, probably including things like homes for everyone, sustainability, people-oriented communities, and ways to improve the quality of life in Kirkland for everyone. Drop in and bring friends, family, and neighbors! Okay to bring kids and food, too. Follow all of Liveable Kirkland's events: On our calendar: https://www.liveablekirkland.org/calendar On Meetup: https://www.meetup.com/liveablekirkland/ And connect with us on Discord, Facebook, and subscribe to our weekly newsletter here: https://www.liveablekirkland.org/join

Event details

item.title: Raised By TV Events Presents: all ages The Simpsons Trivia Night
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Clock-Out Lounge, 4864 Beacon Ave S, Seattle, WA

RAISED By TV brings you:THE SIMPSONS Trivia Night!50 BRAND NEW questions about your favs Springfieldians- covering the first 7 seasons Mon Mar 16th 6:30pm$7 ONLINE ADVANCE: \{plus ticketing fees\}$9 Day of & AT THE DOOR \{plus ticketing fees\}Doors open at 5:00. Trivia starts at 6:30!Tables are first come first serve.Venue is 21\+ after 8pm \*\*PRIZES\*\*The RAISED BY TV gang has put together some killer prize packages. Teams of 1-5 are welcome. Prizes for 1st, 2nd, t have to be good at trivia just come on down to relive some Springfield antics with us. \*\*FOOD & DRINKS\*\*The Clock-Out has bar and full Stevies Famous Pizza food service; one of the things that makes it our favorite place.The show is available on DisneyWe hope you can join RbyTV for a safe night of fun and brains.Brandon Ryan

Event details

item.title: BE LOUD Breakfast
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 8 AM

Description Join us for the BE LOUD Breakfast, an annual fundraising event for the King County Sexual Assault Resource Center \(KCSARC\). This year&'s event is especially meaningful as 2026 marks the 50th anniversary of KCSARC. For fifty years, KCSARC has empowered survivors and communities. This incredible milestone is a testament to you-our community. You have shown up, spoken out, and stood with survivors every step of the way. Today, we face new and urgent challenges. But our mission remains unwavering. Together, we will keep moving forward-LOUDER than ever before. Will you BE LOUD with us? Register now at donate.kcsarc.org/BELOUD26

Event details

item.title: Coworking Tuesdays
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Bothell Kenmore Chamber, 10031 Main Street, Suite A, Bothell, WA

Tuesday Cowork \+ Coffee/Tea \(Weekly\) Need a reason to get out of your inbox cave and into a productive, low-pressure space? Join us every Tuesday for a drop-in coworking morning—bring your laptop, knock out your priorities, and enjoy the energy of working alongside other local professionals. When: Tuesdays | 9:30–11:30 AM | No registration needed Format: Come for the full two hours or pop in when you can Who it’s for: Business owners, remote workers, creatives, students, nonprofit leaders—anyone who wants focused work time \+ light community What to expect: Quiet “get-it-done” work blocks, optional hellos \+ quick intros, and a friendly vibe \(no forced networking\) Location \(through February\): Bothell Visitor Center We’ll rotate locations monthly—same time, same Tuesday rhythm, fresh spaces. Bring: Your laptop \+ whatever you need to work. We have the coffee, tea, and water! Questions? Email sandra@bothellkenmorechamber.org

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Seattle Official St. Patrick's Day Bar Crawl
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Ozzie's, 105 West Mercer Street, Seattle, WA

Seattle Official St. Patrick's Day Bar Crawl! Looking for things to do in Seattle? Join us for our annual St. Paddy's Pub Crawl!

Event details

item.title: Issaquah Alps Trail Running Club #227
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Mountainside Drive Trailhead, Mountainside Dr SW, Issaquah, WA

Meet at the Bullitt Fireplace Trailhead on Mountainside drive. There's plenty of parking along Mountainside drive. Bring a headlamp for the run. Join us after the run for our weekly potluck. Bring a potluck dish with a serving utensils, and your own dish and utensil. Route: Main Route: This route is 6mi with 1600ft of gain. The run starts with most the vert up to the microwave, and heads down Chybinski trail. Route: https://www.strava.com/routes/3290114707713132698 Strava Event: https://www.strava.com/clubs/993715/group\_events/3462646694539309928

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Lynnwood Chamber Luncheon
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Join our March Networking Luncheon, at Fogo de Chao with Special Guests, Lynnwood, WA

item.title: Coffee & Connections: Chamber Member Morning Meet-Up
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 8 AM

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Lunch Ladies
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 11:30 AM

Location: Changes Monthly, 319 4th Ave, Kirkland, WA

Join the ladies in our church every 3rd Thursday of the month for lunch and fellowship! Location rotates, RSVP and check the info center or app for location! Please RSVP so we know how big of a table to reserve!

Event details

item.title: FREE OFFICE AND COFFEE FRIDAYS!
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Spaces- Stadium, 450 Alaskan Way South #Suite 200, Seattle, WA

Join us for the day and treat yourself to one of our cozy, furnished office spaces. The coffee's on us!

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

Event details

item.title: Forterra Fridays
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Crestview Park, Seattle, WA

Forterra Fridays: March 2026 Friday, Mar. 20, 2026, 1:00 pm to 3:30 pm We'reexcited to invite you to volunteer with us at Crestview Park, a beloved community gathering space in Tukwila featuring dog play areas, trails, and natural landscapes. Join us on March 20, from 1:00-3:30 PM, to restore essential habitat by removing ivy and Himalayan blackberries that threaten our local native trees, shrubs, and groundcover. Established native plantsbenefitlocal wildlife, improve water quality, and enhance air quality. You will also gain valuable insights into ecological restoration and our innovative approach to land conservation.We'llstart with a walk of the site and have restoration professionals on hand to answer questions about plants, restoration, and habitat management. What we are doing: Crestview Park provides critical habitat restoration opportunities alongside its popular amenities. By removing invasive ivy and blackberries, we ensure that native plants can thrive, supporting the park's ecological health while maintaining the community spaces that make this park so valued by Tukwila residents. What to bring: Please dress for the weather \(in layers to stay warm/dry\). Snacks, drinks, tools, and gloves will all be provided. Where to park: Park in the Crestview Park parking lot \( 16200 42nd Avenue South\). Signs direct you from the parking lot to where we will meet. Date and Time: Friday, March 20, 2026, 1:00 -3:30 PM Register and learn more here .

Event details

item.title: The Taste of Iceland Festival March 19-21
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Taste of Iceland returns to Seattle from Thursday, March 19, through Saturday, March 21. The cultural festival celebrates Iceland with events across the city showcasing the best of Icelandic culture, including food, music, nature, art, travel, and more. Most events are free and open to the public, including two free concerts, a prix fixe Icelandic dining experience, a comedy show, and a cocktail class. Tickets are required and guests are encouraged to arrive promptly to guarantee entry. Tickets are available on the Taste of Iceland Seattle website.  Taste of Iceland events include: Thursday, March 19 - Saturday, March 21 Icelandic Menu at Palace Kitchen: Ready to taste a plate full of Iceland? Icelandic Chef Ísak Aron Jóhannsson and Palace Kitchen Chef Ron Anderson will prepare a feast inspired by Iceland’s natural beauty and local flavors, including cod from Icelandic Seafood and lamb from Icelandic Lamb. This pop-up dining experience is available each evening beginning at 5:00 PM at Palace Kitchen. Reservations for the prix fixe menu are available at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/icelandic-menu. Wheel of Prizes Presented by Icelandair: Select guests will have the opportunity to spin the Wheel of Prizes for a chance to win amazing prizes from all festival partners during multiple events, including a trip for two to Iceland! Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/wheel-of-prizes. Thursday, March 19 Reykjavík Has Its Own Soundtrack: For one night only, Icelandic multi-instrumentalist, producer, and musician DJ Hermigervill invites Seattle audiences to experience an intimate listening party at Shibuya HiFi. The evening explores the songs that capture what it means to live and grow up in Iceland’s vibrant capital. Hermigervill opens his personal archive for an exclusive journey through Reykjavík’s musical identity, sharing rare recordings and the stories behind them. Presented in the spirit of Reykjavík Music City, the event celebrates a capital where the city helps shape the sound. 7:30 - 9:00 PM at Shibuya HiFi. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/reykjavik-soundtrack. Friday, March 20 Across Iceland: A Guided Cocktail Journey: During this two-hour cocktail class, storyteller and Icelandic guide Ragnar Bragason will lead guests through some of Iceland’s most stunning regions, sharing tales from the road and the places where Iceland Hotel Collection by BERJAYA welcomes travelers. At each stop, Icelandic mixologist, Arnór Gísli, will teach guests to create a signature cocktail inspired by the region's landscape, mood, and character. Three destinations. Three drinks. One unforgettable trip. 4:00 - 6:00 PM at Life on Mars. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/cocktail-journey. Northern Lights in Seattle: Learn about Iceland’s fascinating northern lights—which are expected to shine even brighter in 2026 and 2027—with astronomy and science educator, Sævar "Astro-Sævar" Helgi Bragason, hosted by Icelandia. Sævar will explain why Iceland is the perfect place to view the August 12, 2026, solar eclipse and recommend his favorite tours in Iceland. Guests will also be invited to experience Iceland through virtual reality, with a focus on Icelandia's Northern Lights tours, Glacier Hikes, and ATV tours. 6:00 - 7:30 PM at the National Nordic Museum. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/northern-lights. Iceland Airwaves Off-Venue: Enjoy a free concert curated by Iceland Airwaves, an award-winning music festival in Reykjavík, featuring RAKEL and Spacestation, with DJ Hermigervill spinning Icelandic tunes between sets. RAKEL’s intimate, atmospheric music combines voice and emotion into something quietly powerful. Spacestation blends shimmering rock and textured synths, creating a cosmic energy that feels both nostalgic and fresh. DJ Hermigervill is a multi-instrumentalist, producer, composer, and DJ. Doors open at 7:00 PM at Conor Byrne Co-op. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/iceland-airwaves-off-venue. Saturday, March 21 Draw Iceland’s Stories! Family Workshop with Illustrator Rán Flygenring: Join award-winning Icelandic storyteller and illustrator Rán Flygenring for a playful, inspiring family art workshop where imagination meets Icelandic nature and history. In this workshop, Rán invites children and parents alike to explore storytelling through drawing, doodling, and other creative activities. Families will learn about Iceland’s powerful landscapes, trailblazing leaders, and creative spirit. All ages are welcome, and no prior art experience is required—just curiosity, imagination, and a sense of fun! 11:00 AM - 2:00 PM at the National Nordic Museum. Sign up at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/draw-icelands-stories for a chance to win a signed edition of one of Rán's illustrated children’s books. Iceland Unfiltered: Get ready for an evening of laughter hosted by Icelandair! At this one-night-only comedy event, two Icelanders are flying to Seattle to reveal the quirky truths, cultural oddities, and straight-up strange realities of Iceland. Comedians and storytellers Ragnar Bragason and Bergur Ebbi will share hilarious personal stories, offer wildly unqualified advice, and roast what tourists get wrong…every single time. Each guest will receive a complimentary cocktail infused with local flavors and Blue Lagoon Iceland will provide every guest with a skincare gift. Expect laughter and at least five conversation-starters for your next dinner party. Spoiler: You've definitely been pronouncing Reykjavík wrong. 4:00 - 6:00 PM at The Rabbit Box. Learn more at https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle/iceland-unfiltered. Taste of Iceland was created to celebrate and introduce the traditions and culture of Iceland to North America. For more information on Taste of Iceland, please visit https://www.inspiredbyiceland.com/taste-of-iceland/seattle.

Event details

item.title: Aperitivo e libri
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Mercerwood Shore Club, 4150 E Mercer Way, Mercer Island, WA

The date is set for our next Aperitivo! We will share drinks, foods and stories. Our aperitivo will be joined by Giovanni Menicocci, from Helike Edizioni, who will join us from Italy via zoom to talk about their books Mercerwood Shore Club, 4150 E Mercer Way, Mercer Island Friday, March 20, 2026 6PM - Meet & greet 7PM - Food 8PM - Meet Giovanni Optional donation $25 \(to support Il Punto Seattle\). Venmo or cash accepted at the venue. Please note: Due to the presence of alcohol, this event is for adults only. Registration at the link: https://www.ilpuntoseattle.org/event-6545241

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.
